title: dryopteris boryana
intro: Dryopteris boryana, also known as Bory's fern or Bory's wood fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteris genus. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in the Mediterranean region, particularly in Greece and Turkey. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris boryana include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant, and it is often prepared as a tea or infusion for herbal remedies.
